sibling_stub_pending_scope: STUB-RAG-INDEX-SEAM-DELETION-001 (CORE bbdeba5, DISPATCH-043) — depends on this WO landing first

handoff.reason: Scoping complete: simple_implementation plan with 3 atomic commits + 16 ACs + 5 decision records. Boundary clarification recorded in DR-FROZEN-B (fixture lands in src/integration/rag/__tests__/fixtures/; test stays at __tests__/pipeline/ to preserve git blame + adjacency to Phase 7 chokepoint invariants — surface at execution if ORCHESTRATOR wants the test path moved too). Identity-not-numbers + anti-tautology guard locked. Mutation-guard demonstration is the AC-11 closure evidence.

execution_rules.phase_gates: Phase 1 (fixture authored) gates Phase 2 (test refactor); Phase 2 gates Phase 3 (CI verification + lock-down); each phase requires tsc clean both configs + relevant test green
execution_rules.tsc_invariant: tsc --noEmit clean both configs (tsconfig.json + tsconfig.cli.json) after each commit

hard_constraints: Phase 7 archive MUST NOT be reopened
Boundary: __tests__/pipeline/* (existing seed) + src/integration/rag/__tests__/fixtures/ (new fixture) + minimal CI wiring (vitest config / package.json) ONLY
Production code in src/integration/rag/*.ts MUST NOT change
Frozen fixture must encode the IDENTITY relationship, NOT specific real-world numbers (2415/263 are anchor numbers but not load-bearing)
Anti-tautology guard required: expected counts computed from loaded graph.json (input), not hardcoded literals or runtime output
Distinctness assertion stays in form `not.toBe(otherGrain)` — assert relationship, not specific numbers
Existing __tests__/pipeline/indexing-gate-invariant.test.ts AC-05 block (lines 98-241) is the SEED — refactor in place, do NOT duplicate at new path
Phase 7 chokepoint INVARIANT block (lines 32-70 of same file) untouched
tsc --noEmit clean both configs after each commit
Cultural validator scope-mode + tracking validator scope-mode PASS clean (acknowledge pre-existing 14/15 stubs.json schema drift per STUB-044)

communication_log.summary: WO-RAG-INDEX-FROZEN-FIXTURE-DUAL-AC-INVARIANTS-001 authored under DISPATCH-045 (high priority post-bulletproof-pivot-1 backlog, concept #2 of 6). plan_type=simple_implementation; 3 atomic commits planned (fixture + test refactor + CI verification); 16 ACs; +30 to +80 net LOC target. Predecessor stub STUB-RAG-INDEX-FROZEN-FIXTURE-DUAL-AC-INVARIANTS-001 (CORE 1cc4acb) is the source; predecessor WO-RAG-INDEX-SINGLE-ANALYZER-SLICE-001 (a19249a) is the substrate that introduced dual-AC framing. Boundary clarification recorded in DR-FROZEN-B (fixture lands in src/integration/rag/__tests__/fixtures/, test stays at __tests__/pipeline/ — surface during execution if ORCHESTRATOR wants test moved too). Identity-not-numbers + anti-tautology + distinctness-as-relationship all locked in DR-FROZEN-C/D. Standing by for ORCHESTRATOR to dispatch CODEREF-CORE for execution.

WO-RAG-INDEX-FROZEN-FIXTURE-DUAL-AC-INVARIANTS-001 complete (closeout-ready). Three atomic commits landed: e17561b (C1 fixture authoring +144 LOC), 3adf32a (C2 test refactor +98/-107 = -9 net LOC + vitest config exclude), 6fafa98 (C3 verification-only with MUTATION-GUARD-EVIDENCE.md +76 LOC). Total +211 net LOC (overshoot vs +30-80 target driven by fixture data + evidence file content; reported truthfully). AC-01..AC-14 PASS, AC-15/AC-16 deferred to /close-workorder. T3.1 hit halt-and-report point: under DR-FROZEN-C input-derived expected, original plan recipe (flip ONE node) does NOT fail test because dual-AC identity holds in mutated fixture; surfaced 3 paths to ORCHESTRATOR. ORCHESTRATOR ruled Path A (flip both missing nodes of mod0). Mutation captured as `expected 3 to be 2` cross-check FAIL at line 175 of test, vitest exits 1 (CI-blocking confirmed), mutation reverted, evidence in MUTATION-GUARD-EVIDENCE.md. Verification: tsc clean both configs at every phase gate; vitest 263/264 PASS in pipeline+integration sweep (1 pre-existing baseline failure: chunk-converter:243 maxSourceCodeLength, unrelated, documented in predecessor WO closeouts); default `vitest run` includes AC-05 dual-identity test. Production code in src/integration/rag/*.ts (excluding __tests__/) untouched (AC-13). AnalyzerService source untouched (AC-14). Phase 7 chokepoint INVARIANT block lines 32-70 untouched (AC-10). Bulletproof concept #2 of 6 shipped. Standing by for ORCHESTRATOR to dispatch SKILLS for cross-project /close-workorder.

decisions_log.rationale: DISPATCH-045 dispatch payload directed plan_type=simple_implementation OR phased_implementation depending on whether fixture-shape decision needs an investigation phase. SKILLS judged no investigation phase needed: the seed test (__tests__/pipeline/indexing-gate-invariant.test.ts:98-241) is a working reference, surface area is enumerable from a single grep, and the only real decision (refactor seed in place vs author new test) is recorded inline in DR-FROZEN-B rather than dragged through a phase. Full /create-workorder Step 4-7 chain ran.

decisions_log.ruling: Plan T3.1's original recipe ('flip ONE hs=missing node → test FAILS') was inconsistent with DR-FROZEN-C input-derived expected. Single-node flip yields a mutated fixture where the dual-AC identity STILL HOLDS (re-derived expected matches actual). 3 paths surfaced: (A) flip BOTH missing nodes of one file, breaking file-grain invariant 3→2; (B) mutate validation-report.json (sentinel-side); (C) hardcoded supplementary assertion (rejected — violates DR-FROZEN-D). ORCHESTRATOR ruled (A): substrate-side mutation is the correct shape for catching production drift. NOT a new DR — DR-FROZEN-C still governs; this is plan-spec clarification inline.
decisions_log.rationale: Path A demonstrates the substrate-vs-baseline mechanic: graph.json (substrate) disagreeing with validation-report.json (baseline) is what the cross-check at test line 175 catches. Sentinel-side mutation (Path B) only proves the two files agree, not that production code honors the identity. Hardcoded supplementary assertion (Path C) directly violates DR-FROZEN-D identity-not-numbers principle.

decisions_log.ruling: Added `**/__tests__/fixtures/**` to the vitest exclude glob in vitest.config.ts. Necessary because the fixture src/*.ts files (legitimate non-test TypeScript modules) were picked up by vitest's `**/__tests__/**/*.ts` include glob and triggered 'No test suite found' false positives.
decisions_log.rationale: Falls within the dispatch's 'minimal CI wiring' boundary clause. 3-line addition, scoped to the exclude list. No production code change; no test logic change.
